$\gdef\xb{x\textrm{b}}$ # $\xb \textrm{ } \cdot \textrm{ the } anything \textrm{ book}$ $\xb$ is a versatile notebook that can become anything: a web page, a report, a technical document or even an interactive application! All this within a Web-standards-based platform. --- - cell - attributes: data-type, data-active - output - created by OutputContext.create_cell_output() - attributes: data-source-media-type, data-source-element - css class: xb-cell-output - XbManager.singleton - controls data-cell-view attribute on document.documentElement (:root) - ocx - element - renderer

\gdef\xb{x\textrm{b}}

xb ⋅ the anything book\xb \textrm{ } \cdot \textrm{ the } anything \textrm{ book}xb ⋅ the anything book

xb\xbxb is a versatile notebook that can become anything: a web page, a report, a technical document or even an interactive application!

All this within a Web-standards-based platform.

  • cell
    • attributes: data-type, data-active
  • output
    • attributes: data-source-media-type, data-source-element
    • css class: xb-cell-output
  • XbManager.singleton
    • controls data-cell-view attribute on document.documentElement
  • ocx
    • element
  • renderer

=== XB DESIGN NOTES ===

editor renderer ocx (output context)

document_manager activity_manager command_manager

settings

locus of activity management candidates:

  • XbManager.singleton
  • ocx
  • renderer
  • cell
  • output_element choose: XbManager.singleton and ocx

"cell" elements

  • id
  • css class: "cell"
  • dataset attributes: -- type: media-type, e.g., "text/plain", "text/html", "text/javascipt", "text/typescript", "application/json", "text/x-tex", "text/x-latex", ...

"output" elements

  • class: "xb-cell-output"
  • dataset attributes: -- source-element: source element (cell) id -- source-media-type

see: https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml

alternative terms: renderer, translator, converter, interpreter, compiler, evaluator

reactive? typescript? Signals?

immutable? virtual dom?
